signals.recognition: A white illuminated numeral on a dark indicator panel.
signals.meaning: The displayed numeral multiplied by 10 is the maximum speed in km/h from the signal through the following points and crossings area.
playerAction: Read the white numeral and multiply it by 10.
Do not exceed that speed from the signal.
Continue to observe any more restrictive timetable, cab-signalling or lineside limit.
notes: The listed values are rendering support, not a claim that every value appears on every Train Sim World route.
Route observations must record which numerals are actually represented.

sources.title: Eisenbahn-Signalordnung and DB Signalbuch meaning of Zs 3

signals.recognition: A yellow illuminated numeral on a dark indicator panel.
signals.meaning: Expect a Zs 3 with the same numeral. The numeral multiplied by 10 is the speed in km/h to be observed from that later signal or defined point.
playerAction: Read the yellow numeral and multiply it by 10.
Begin braking in time to reach that speed at the associated Zs 3.
Acknowledge PZB when an associated 1000 Hz influence is active.

requiresWithKs.reason: Flashing green draws attention to the speed announcement.

notes: PZB action is conditional on the simulated magnet state and must be verified per route.
The listed values are rendering support, not confirmed TSW route coverage.

sources.title: Eisenbahn-Signalordnung and DB Signalbuch meaning of Zs 3v
